translation_findings.issue: Inconsistent name-order convention across the two tables for the same JP person 上峰亜衣 — first table uses Western order 'Ai Kamine' (given+surname), second table uses Japanese order 'Kamine Ai' (surname+given). Other names (Taro Yamada, Aiko Suzuki, Hanako Tanaka, Ichiro Ito, Haruko Sato) consistently use Western order. The 'Kamine Ai' entry is the outlier.
translation_findings.suggested_fix: Change second-table row3 en from 'Kamine Ai' to 'Ai Kamine' to match the Western-order convention used by all other names on the page.

translation_findings.issue: JP source has internally inconsistent phone-number digit counts between the pre- and post-normalization tables for the same row (e.g., 090-9999999X vs 090-99999999X). zh/en faithfully mirror jp, so this is a source defect not a translation defect — flagged for awareness only.
translation_findings.suggested_fix: No translation fix needed; consider raising as source/OCR cleanup ticket.

narrative: Three database-normalization sample tables plus two terms (正規化 / E-R図). Translations are semantically faithful. One MED inconsistency: en name order for 上峰亜衣 differs between the two tables (Ai Kamine vs Kamine Ai). One LOW source-mirrored numerical inconsistency (phone digit count) — not a translation defect.
